content:
- User asked to make all three docs story tasks high priority.
- Searched local Ralph conventions and found task selection expects `<priority>` tags such as `<priority>high</priority>`.
- Added `<priority>high</priority>` directly under the title line in all three story-docs-k2-expansion task files and verified the insertion.

- Read progress log, .ralph/current_tasks.md, and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Checked all active candidate task files directly for <priority> and <blocked_by>; no blocker tags were present and done list was empty.
- No unblocked failing bug tasks existed. Highest-priority failing tasks were the docs high-priority tasks. Chose the first docs-story task because it generates the chapter content that tasks 02 and 03 explicitly depend on for linking and final verification.
- Wrote .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Read Ralph progress, current task, and saw this task was already in NOW EXECUTE state, so I executed rather than replanning.
- Ran five parallel k2-docs-loop choose-doc lanes. Needed rerolls because one lane returned a malformed tool-call artifact and another duplicated an existing target path; final unique targets were docs/src/reference/cli-commands.md, docs/src/reference/pgtuskmasterctl-cli.md, docs/src/reference/cli.md, docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.md, and docs/src/reference/cli-pgtuskmasterctl.md.
- Gathered requested raw files plus wrote verbose extra context files at docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/cli-surface-summary.md and docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/runtime-config-summary.md.
- Ran five parallel prepare-draft lanes successfully. Drafts landed under docs/draft/docs/src/reference/.
- Did factual review. Marked unsupported claims in runtime-configuration draft and an unsupported exit-code line in pgtuskmasterctl-cli draft with // todo comments per skill workflow.
- Ran the mandated revise_prompt pass on all five drafts. Results were mixed: cli-commands.revised.md and pgtuskmasterctl-cli.revised.md are usable-ish but wrapped in fenced-code blocks; cli.revised.md and cli-pgtuskmasterctl.revised.md are malformed tool-call artifacts instead of markdown; runtime-configuration.revised.md removed todo comments but reintroduced several unsupported validation claims. I did not move any revised files into docs/src because that would publish clearly bad artifacts.
- Should do next: resume from these draft artifacts, decide whether to rerun only the broken revise lanes or manually do the minimal artifact cleanup/factual pruning allowed by the workflow, then final-check and move only clean pages into docs/src before ending another batch.

- Read progress log and current task pointer.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md.
- Opening task file now to determine whether the task is in planning, verification, or execution state.

- Task file is already in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Not replanning; loading the k2-docs-loop skill and checking current published docs coverage to decide whether another five-lane batch is required.
- Previous batch left several revised drafts malformed or factually weak, so I need to either salvage or rerun only the broken lanes.

- Coverage gate failed immediately: docs/src/explanation and docs/src/reference do not exist, so another batch is required.
- Inspecting docs/, draft/, and tmp/ artifacts from the previous batch to identify which lanes can be finalized versus which need reruns.

- Published a source-backed reference page at docs/src/reference/pgtuskmasterctl-cli.md based on src/cli/args.rs, src/cli/client.rs, src/cli/output.rs, src/cli/error.rs, and src/bin/pgtuskmasterctl.rs.
- Updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md to expose the new Reference section and set the task status to in_progress while keeping passes=false.
- Coverage is still obviously incomplete because explanation docs are still absent and only one reference page is published; next batch should continue with new choose-doc lanes or salvage the remaining drafted reference/runtime-config pages if they can be made fully source-supported.

- Continued the NOW EXECUTE docs batch for .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md.
- Coverage check still showed docs/src/explanation missing and reference still thin, so I ran five parallel choose-doc lanes again. K2 collapsed almost entirely onto docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.md again; only one reroll produced a second usable unique target, docs/src/explanation/architecture.md.
- Gathered requested raw files for runtime config and architecture, wrote exhaustive support notes at docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/runtime-config-deep-summary.md and docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/architecture-deep-summary.md, then ran prepare-draft for both lanes.
- Factual review found both first-pass drafts had unsupported claims. I inserted // todo markers and ran the mandated revise_prompt pass.
- Runtime-config revise failed badly again and returned a tool-call artifact instead of markdown at docs/draft/docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.revised.md, so I did not publish it.
- Architecture revise succeeded well enough to salvage. I manually cleaned artifact placeholders into simple mermaid diagrams, published docs/src/explanation/architecture.md, and updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md to add an Explanation section.
- Task is still not done: docs/src/reference remains too thin and runtime configuration reference is still missing despite repeated K2 attempts. Next engineer should probably keep this task in NOW EXECUTE, salvage the runtime-config page from the prepared context or rerun only that lane plus more choose-doc attempts for additional reference/how-to pages.

- Resumed Ralph docs task from .ralph/current_task.txt and confirmed the active task file is still in NOW EXECUTE.
- Loading only the k2-docs-loop skill instructions needed to run the next five-lane batch exactly as written.
- Next step is to execute the docs batch, publish only source-backed pages, append progress again, and stop unless coverage is complete.

- Coverage check showed docs/src still has only one real page in each Diataxis section, so task remains in NOW EXECUTE and another five-lane batch is required.
- Launched five parallel k2-docs-loop choose-doc lanes and all returned successfully into docs/tmp/k2-batch/20260308-batch2-lane*.choose.md.
- Reading the lane outputs now to extract unique targets and exact requested raw files before prepare-draft.

- Five parallel choose-doc lanes all collapsed again onto the same missing target: docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.md.
- Gathered src/config/schema.rs, src/config/defaults.rs, src/config/parser.rs, both docker runtime.toml examples, and wrote docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/runtime-config-deep-summary.md to give K2 exhaustive raw context.
- Ran prepare-draft for runtime-configuration, reviewed the draft for factual accuracy, inserted todo markers for unsupported claims, ran the mandated revise prompt, then minimally cleaned the revised output.
- Published docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.md and added it to docs/src/SUMMARY.md.
- Task is still not complete: tutorial/how-to/explanation/reference are still not all meaningfully populated. Next engineer should continue in NOW EXECUTE, likely with a fresh choose-doc batch after runtime-configuration is now published.

- Read the active docs task and confirmed it remains in NOW EXECUTE, so I must execute rather than replan.
- Loaded the k2-docs-loop skill and the ask-k2 helper instructions because this task requires K2-led choose-doc and drafting lanes.
- Next I am checking published docs coverage at startup; if all four Diataxis sections are not clearly meaningfully populated, I will launch another five parallel choose-doc lanes.

- Startup coverage gate failed again: docs/src currently has tutorial=1, explanation=1, how-to=1, reference=2 real pages, which is not enough to call the docs meaningfully populated.
- Per task instructions I am proceeding directly into another five-lane choose-doc batch rather than replanning.
- I need lane-local choose-doc artifacts first, then I will gather only the exact raw files each lane requests and prepare five drafts in parallel.

- Five fresh choose-doc lanes all converged on docs/src/how-to/perform-switchover.md again.
- I cannot safely run prepare-draft in parallel against the same target path because docs/draft/docs/src/how-to/perform-switchover.md and the tmp prompt path would collide between lanes.
- Doing a minimal four-lane reroll now so the batch remains truly independent instead of fabricating lane-local target names.

- Fresh choose-doc work produced one strong new how-to target (perform-switchover) and one new reference target (pgtuskmaster-cli); repeated collisions prevented a clean five-unique-target batch.
- I gathered the requested source files for both recovered targets and am now filling a small number of exact gaps before writing verbose extra-context files.
- Next step is prepare-draft for the distinct targets, then factual review, K2 revision, minimal cleanup, publish, update SUMMARY if needed, append progress, and stop.

- Wrote verbose extra-context files for perform-switchover and pgtuskmaster-cli so K2 gets very exhaustive source-backed context.
- Starting prepare-draft for the two distinct targets in parallel, each with only the exact raw files plus its verbose support note.
- After draft generation I need a skeptical factual review before asking K2 for the mandatory revise pass.

- Executed another docs batch in NOW EXECUTE.
- K2 collapsed almost all choose-doc lanes onto perform-switchover again; after rerolls I recovered two distinct targets worth publishing: docs/src/how-to/perform-switchover.md and docs/src/reference/pgtuskmaster-cli.md.
- Gathered requested raw files, added exhaustive verbose context, ran prepare-draft, did skeptical factual review with // todo markers, ran revise_prompt, then minimally cleaned the outputs and published the two source-backed pages.
- Updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md to include the new how-to and reference pages.
- Did not run tests or validation commands beyond `cargo run --bin ... --help`, because the task file explicitly forbids test/lint/check runs for this docs-only batch.
- Task is still not complete: docs/src/tutorial remains at one page and explanation is still only architecture, so coverage is still too thin to set passes=true. Next engineer should continue in NOW EXECUTE with another choose-doc batch, likely pushing harder on tutorial and explanation coverage while also adding more how-to/reference depth.

- Post-commit hook caught a docs-lint policy failure: json fenced blocks are not allowed in how-to docs.
- Fixed the two response examples in docs/src/how-to/perform-switchover.md to use text fences instead.
- Verifying with make docs-lint now before creating a follow-up commit and pushing.

- Resumed the active docs task from .ralph/current_task.txt and confirmed it is still in NOW EXECUTE.
- Coverage check still failed: tutorial and explanation each have only one published page, so passes must remain false.
- Ran five parallel k2-docs-loop choose-doc lanes into docs/tmp/k2-batch/20260308-batch3/ and recovered five distinct targets:
  - docs/src/how-to/bootstrap-cluster.md
  - docs/src/tutorial/observing-failover.md
  - docs/src/how-to/handle-primary-failure.md
  - docs/src/explanation/failure-modes.md
  - docs/src/reference/http-api.md
- Validated that the requested source files exist; next step is to gather only those exact raw files, write exhaustive verbose context notes per lane, run prepare-draft in parallel, then do skeptical factual review and K2 revise pass before publishing anything.

- New request is a source-grounded fact gathering pass for docs/src/how-to/handle-primary-failure.md only.
- I read prior progress and will inspect only the six user-approved source files, with line-numbered output, no edits.
- Need to answer explicitly whether the exact /ha/state JSON fields and log messages during primary failure handling are directly supported by those files.

- Read prior progress and restricted scope to the seven requested files only.
- Initial pass found bootstrap evidence in ha decision flow, DCS store helpers, schema defaults, runtime example, startup harness etcd checks, and observer assertions.
- Next I am extracting exact zero-state bootstrap and join sequencing with line references for docs/src/how-to/bootstrap-cluster.md.

- Pulled exact line-numbered excerpts for decide.rs, schema.rs, runtime.toml, startup harness, and observer.
- Evidence now shows: first node waits until it becomes bootstrap primary; subsequent nodes require replicator/rewinder roles on that primary; etcd init and config keys are asserted after first-node bootstrap.
- I am checking for any remaining join-order or zero-state nuance before writing the factual summary.

- Gathered source-grounded facts for observing failover docs from the exact user-approved files only.
- Confirmed debug snapshot shape, DCS trust states, HA decision labels/details, cluster topology used by docs/tests, and startup harness behavior.
- Important limitation: the named top-level test files are wrappers and the allowed harness files do not expose the actual per-test failure injection calls, only the proxy-based partition setup and cleanup helpers like pg_ctl_stop_immediate/task abort/shutdown.
- Next if asked, I should either summarize this limitation explicitly in docs notes or request permission to read the support test modules where failure injection likely lives.

- Read only the six requested files with line numbers.
- Extracted primary-failure transition facts from src/ha/decide.rs: primary outage -> rewinding, foreign leader -> fencing, no quorum -> fail-safe, fencing success -> waiting DCS trusted, restored quorum from fail-safe primary -> primary decision path.
- Confirmed the requested files do not contain direct runtime log emission calls for primary failure handling; only observer/test error strings and recent-sample formatting are directly visible.

- Read only the six user-specified files for a source-grounded docs fact pass: src/ha/decide.rs, src/dcs/state.rs, src/dcs/etcd_store.rs, src/ha/decision.rs, tests/ha/support/observer.rs, src/ha/actions.rs.
- Extracted exact HA transition logic with line references for non-quorum fail-safe routing, primary Postgres outage behavior, fencing completion, and fail-safe exit/release behavior.
- Confirmed the explicit source-backed answer for the extra question: when DCS remains healthy/full quorum but local PostgreSQL is unreachable for an extended period, the node stays in WaitingPostgresReachable if it is not currently primary, but a local primary that still holds leadership releases the leader lease with reason postgres_unreachable and moves into Rewinding; after that it either rewinds/base-backups against an available healthy primary or remains in Rewinding/NoChange until one appears. No allowed source shows an automatic timeout-based escalation beyond those states.
- Also captured DCS trust evaluation rules and observer invariants: etcd unhealthy -> NotTrusted, stale/missing member metadata -> FailSafe, all-node FailSafe samples counted, and multi-primary observations rejected.
- Next step if context continues: paste the concise factual summary into the docs lane or use it to review a draft of docs/src/explanation/failure-modes.md without adding unsupported claims.

- New task: source-grounded fact gathering for docs/src/reference/http-api.md, no edits.
- Read only the six approved files and extracted route table, auth behavior, HTTP response builders, debug verbose payload fields, snapshot shape, and CLI-observed request/response examples.
- Key boundary: complete endpoint list/methods/auth/status are source-backed from src/api/worker.rs; response schemas are fully source-backed for /ha/state, /debug/verbose, /debug/snapshot (text debug dump), /debug/ui (HTML), and accepted=true responses, but fallback endpoint body schemas and exact JSON enum wire format for every ha_decision variant are not fully defined in the approved files.

- Finished source gathering from only the requested files.
- Final output will summarize bootstrap from zero state: required runtime fields, DCS init/config key expectations, first-node primary election, pre-join replicator/rewinder setup, and follower join behavior with line references.
- No files edited.

- User asked to fix `git checkout master` failing with `this operation must be run in a work tree` and to remove worktrees while keeping the branch.
- Confirmed current repo path has files checked out but Git is configured as bare: `core.bare=true`, `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree=false`, HEAD points to refs/heads/master.
- Found one linked worktree at `/home/joshazimullah.linux/.t3/worktrees/pgtuskmaster_rust/t3code-e3c77c0d` on branch `main`; next step is to convert this repo back to non-bare, remove the linked worktree, and verify branch checkout/status.

- Restored the repo at /home/joshazimullah.linux/work_mounts/patroni_rewrite/pgtuskmaster_rust from broken bare+worktree admin mode to a normal Git work tree by changing `.git/config` from `core.bare=true` to `core.bare=false` and removing `extensions.worktreeConfig`.
- Verified the current checkout is now a proper work tree on branch `master`; `git checkout master` succeeds and `git rev-parse` reports `is-bare=false`, `is-inside-work-tree=true`.
- Preserved the dirty Ralph bookkeeping from the linked `main` worktree by stashing it as `stash@{0}: On main: pre-remove-linked-worktree`, then removed the linked worktree path `/home/joshazimullah.linux/.t3/worktrees/pgtuskmaster_rust/t3code-e3c77c0d`.
- Branch refs were kept: `main` still exists locally at 68b114ad0d9d728443e828b1eb4d512ca0525afd, and `master` remains checked out here.

- New user request: push the preserved `t3code/docker-cluster-debug-access` branch and open a PR without disturbing existing unstaged changes in the current checkout.
- Verified current working tree on `master` is dirty and must stay untouched. Verified branch relation: `master...t3code/docker-cluster-debug-access` is `6 1`, so the branch is one commit ahead of an outdated base and needs to be replayed on top of current master before push/PR.
- The branch tip commit is `68b114a` (`Add low-priority cluster and switchover tasks`), containing only three new `.ralph/tasks/...` files. Next step is to synthesize an equivalent commit on top of current `master` without checkout, then push that branch ref and create the PR through available GitHub auth.

- Replayed commit `68b114a` onto current `master` without touching the dirty checkout by using an alternate Git index and `git commit-tree`; updated `refs/heads/t3code/docker-cluster-debug-access` to new tip `e4b1ce7` so it is now exactly one commit on top of current master.
- Pushed `t3code/docker-cluster-debug-access` to origin with upstream tracking from the current repo while leaving the unstaged `master` changes untouched.
- Used GitHub credentials from the repo's configured credential helper to create the PR via the GitHub REST API because `gh` was not available locally.
- PR created successfully: https://github.com/djosh34/pgtuskmaster_rust/pull/2

- Continued the docs task in NOW EXECUTE, ran a fresh five-lane choose-doc batch, and got five distinct targets: bootstrap-cluster how-to, observing-failover tutorial, handle-primary-failure how-to, failure-modes explanation, and http-api reference.
- Wrote exhaustive lane-local context notes under docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/, ran five prepare-draft passes in parallel, then did a skeptical factual review and marked unsupported claims with // todo comments.
- The mandated K2 revise pass failed across all five lanes and returned tool-call artifacts instead of markdown. I salvaged the first-pass drafts manually by stripping unsupported commands, timing promises, and placeholder artifacts without redesigning the pages.
- Published five new docs pages to docs/src/, updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md, and ran `make docs-lint` successfully after fixing this repo's docs fence policy violations (no json fences in these sections and no unlabeled fences).
- Coverage now looks meaningfully populated across tutorial, explanation, how-to, and reference, so I marked the task file completed with passes=true. Next step is task switch, commit, push, and quit.
